_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Develop/integrate cybersecurity designs for systems and networks with multilevel security requirements or requirements for the processing of multiple classification levels of data primarily applicable to government organizations (e.g., UNCLASSIFIED, SECRET, and TOP SECRET).
reference_document_element_description: Ability to design security and privacy architectures that enable data utility and AI innovation while maintaining protection levels appropriate to the data sensitivity and regulatory context.
comments: DCWF task superset of WIDAI SP domain. SP-A-002 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: SP-S-008 (SP, cosine: 0.520), SP-T-010 (SP, cosine: 0.492).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 7.3/10 (raw 0.729). Shared vocabulary: [data, levels, security]. DCWF-only terms [applicable, classification, cybersecurity, designs, develop, government] vs WIDAI-only terms [ability, ai, appropriate, architectures, context, design] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.850 (P=0.834, R=0.867). R exceeds P by 0.032 — the WIDAI KSA's 26 words are well-captured by the DCWF element's 33 words, but the DCWF element contains broader scope not fully reflected in the matched WIDAI KSA.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.8%). "Develop/integrate cybersecurity designs for systems and networks with multilevel" and "Ability to design security and privacy architectures that enable data utility an" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.572 < STS 0.729 (gap -0.157). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [applicable, classification, cybersecurity] and [ability, ai, appropriate] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 13.0%. Shared tokens: [data, levels, security]. DCWF-only: [applicable, classification, cybersecurity, designs, develop, government]. WIDAI-only: [ability, ai, appropriate, architectures, context, design].
